sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
ascii_grid = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("=== N part area ===")
for r in range(28, 48):
    row_str = ascii_grid[r]
    n_cols = [c for c in range(64) if row_str[c] == 'N']
    if n_cols:
        print(f"Row {r}: N at cols {min(n_cols)}-{max(n_cols)}")
print("\n=== Red dots ===")
for r in range(64):
    for c in range(64):
        if ascii_grid[r][c] == 'r':
            print(f"  ({r}, {c})")
print("\n=== N part segmentation ===")
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] == 'N' and node['id'] < 30:
        print(f"N: id={node['id']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, boundary={node['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- N vertical bar: rows 31-47, cols 39-41 (17 rows x 3 cols)
The yellow bar is 3 rows tall and 30 cols wide. The L-shape's horizontal bar is 7 rows tall and 12 cols wide. They don't match.

But the yellow bar is controlled by the Y-shapes (horizontal expansion/shrinking), and the N bar is controlled by the N-shapes (vertical expansion/shrinking). The sky blue bar is controlled by the C-shapes (vertical expansion/shrinking), and the orange part is controlled by the C-shapes (horizontal expansion/shrinking).

- Yellow container (Y-shapes): controls the yellow part (horizontal)
